articles.description: Israel launched a daylight attack Saturday on Iran's capital, with a cloud of smoke rising from the city's downtown near the offices of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.title: Live updates: Israel confirms attack on Iran as explosions are heard in Tehran

articles.author: Curtis Yee, Frank Griffiths, Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, David Bryan, Brian P. D. Hannon, Lorian Belanger
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel struck Iran on Saturday. President Donald Trump said Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead, telling Iranians to take back their country.
articles.title: Live updates: Iranian state media confirms death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei

articles.author: Curtis Yee, Frank Griffiths, Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, David Bryan, Brian P. D. Hannon, Lorian Belanger
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel struck Iran on Saturday. President Donald Trump said Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead, telling Iranians to take back their country.
articles.title: Live updates: Iranian state media confirms death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei

articles.author: Jon Gambrell
articles.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who assembled theocratic power in Iran over the decades as its supreme leader and sought to turn it into a regional powerhouse, has died. He was 86. Iranian state media reported the death early Sunday, after a major attack launched by Israel and the United States. U.S. President Donald Trump said hours earlier that Khamenei had been killed in the joint operation. Khamenei dramatically remolded the Islamic Republic since he took the reins after the death of Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989. He brought the country into confrontation with Israel and the United States over its nuclear program while crushing democracy protesters at home.
articles.title: Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead at 86

articles.author: Aamer Madhani, Josh Boak
articles.description: Saturday’s military operation against Iran shows the dramatic evolution in risk tolerance that President Donald Trump has made in a matter of months. It was only last June that Trump had drawn a bright red line in rejecting an Israeli proposal to target Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. At Israel’s urging during its 12-day war with Iran, Trump had agreed to deploy B-2 bombers to pummel three key Iranian nuclear sites, but worried that killing the cleric could destabilize the region. With Saturday’s strikes, those guardrails were tossed aside. Trump triumphantly announced the Ayatollah's death, part of a battle plan that he and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had greenlit.
articles.title: How Trump decided to abandon talks and go to war with Iran

articles.author: Lisa Mascaro
articles.description: Key members of Congress are demanding a swift vote on a war powers resolution that would restrain President Donald Trump’s military attack on Iran. Democrats and some Republicans argue the president must seek approval from Congress for what they warn is a potentially illegal campaign that risks pulling the United States into a deeper Middle East conflict. Both the House and Senate had already drafted war powers resolutions long before the strikes Saturday against Iran. The GOP leadership largely backs Trump's decision. Any war powers vote would largely be symbolic because Trump could veto such a resolution. The Constitution gives Congress the power to declare war.
articles.title: War powers debate in Congress intensifies after Trump's strikes on Iran

articles.description: US military releases videos said to show wave of strikes on Iran
articles.title: US military releases videos said to show wave of strikes on Iran

articles.description: World leaders are responding cautiously after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran raised fears of a wider war.
articles.title: World leaders react to US and Israeli strikes on Iran as fears grow of a wider war

articles.description: In a rare and colorful exchange, the representatives of the United States and Iran exchanged warnings and direct rebuffs toward the end of the emergency session on Iran as military aggression between their countries risked spilling into a regional war.
articles.title: Iranian and US diplomats clash in tense exchanges at UN Security Council meeting
